name: Ankou
lore: The personification of death; collects souls in a creaking cart.

name: Apsat
lore: Caucasian spirit of birds and fish; protects hunters.

name: Banshee
lore: A wailing woman whose cries foretell a death in the family.

name: Barbegazi
lore: Alpine mountain men with giant feet used as snowshoes.

name: Barstukas
lore: Tiny earth men who live under elder trees and bring grain.

name: Basajaun
lore: The Lord of the Forest who taught humans metallurgy.

name: Bean-nighe
lore: The Washer at the Ford who cleans bloodied shrouds.

name: Bwbach
lore: A Welsh brownie that helps with chores but hates dissent.

name: Clurichaun
lore: A surly, drunken cousin of the leprechaun who raids cellars.

name: Domovoy
lore: The protective spirit of the Slavic household and hearth.

name: Duergar
lore: Malicious, dark-dwelling dwarves of the English hills.

name: Dwarf
lore: Subterranean smiths known for crafting legendary items.

name: Gnome
lore: Earth-dwelling spirits that guard underground treasure.

name: Haltija
lore: A guardian spirit assigned to a person, home, or forest.

name: Kallikantzaros
lore: Goblins that surface during the 12 days of Christmas.

name: Kikimora
lore: A female house spirit; helpful if clean, chaotic if not.

name: Korrigans
lore: Beautiful but dangerous fae who dance in stone circles.

name: Leprechaun
lore: A solitary cobbler who guards a pot of gold at the rainbow.

name: Leshy
lore: A forest guardian who can grow as tall as a tree.

name: Lutin
lore: A prankish French spirit that tangles hair into elf-locks.

name: Aitvaras
lore: A household spirit that looks like a rooster or a fireball.

name: Afanc
lore: A lake-dwelling beast that causes massive, drowning floods.

name: Black Shuck
lore: A spectral black dog with glowing eyes; an omen of death.

name: Càssait-Bheinn
lore: A giant, elusive bird of the Scottish Highlands.

name: Cirein-cròin
lore: A sea serpent so large it feeds on seven whales.

name: Fenrir
lore: The monstrous wolf of Norse myth, destined to kill Odin.

name: Iku-Turso
lore: A terrifying, multi-tentacled sea monster of the North.

name: Kraken
lore: A colossal sea beast that creates ship-sinking whirlpools.

name: Lavellan
lore: A rat-like water creature with highly poisonous skin.

name: Marool
lore: A malevolent sea fish with multiple eyes and a finned crest.

name: Nessie
lore: The long-necked, elusive resident of Loch Ness.

name: Otso
lore: The sacred Spirit of the Bear in Finnish mythology.

name: Sleipnir
lore: Odin’s eight-legged stallion, the fastest of all horses.

name: Water Horse
lore: Predatory horses that lure people into the water to drown.

name: Wolpertinger
lore: A small Bavarian hybrid (rabbit with antlers and wings).

name: Centaur
lore: A creature with the upper body of a human and the body of a horse.

name: Satyr
lore: A forest spirit with the upper body of a man and the legs of a goat.

name: Minotaur
lore: A monster with the head of a bull and the body of a man, kept in a labyrinth.

name: Harpy
lore: A predatory creature with the head of a woman and the body of a bird.

name: Scylla
lore: A sea monster with multiple heads and tentacles emerging from her waist.

name: Mermaid
lore: An aquatic entity with a human upper body and a fish-like tail.

name: Sirena
lore: Similar to a mermaid, often lure sailors with enchanting songs.

name: Sphinx
lore: A creature with the head of a human, body of a lion, and wings.

name: Manticore
lore: A beast with a human face, lion body, and a venomous scorpion tail.

name: Faun
lore: A peaceful woodland spirit, half-human and half-goat.

name: Gorgon
lore: Humanoid with snakes for hair; their gaze turns living things to stone.

name: Ichthyocentaur
lore: A sea-being with a human torso, horse forelegs, and a fish tail.

name: Larnia
lore: A female demon who devours children; often depicted as half-serpent.

name: Nixie
lore: Water spirits that appear human but can take on fish-like traits.

name: Sileni
lore: Older, wiser, and often drunken companions of Dionysus.

name: Triton
lore: A merman of the deep, often acting as a messenger for the sea gods.

name: Cynocephaly
lore: A race of humans with the heads of dogs, often found in early travelogues.

name: Each-Uisge
lore: A shapeshifting water spirit that often takes a handsome human form.

name: Melusine
lore: A feminine spirit of fresh water, often appearing with a serpent tail.

name: Rusalka
lore: A vengeful water spirit that lures men into the water to drown.

name: Griffin
lore: A creature with the body of a lion and the head and wings of an eagle.

name: Hippogriff
lore: The legendary offspring of a griffin and a mare, with a horse's hindquarters.

name: Chimera
lore: A fire-breathing hybrid featuring a lion’s head, a goat’s body, and a serpent tail.

name: Pegasus
lore: A divine winged stallion, usually white, born from the blood of Medusa.

name: Questing Beast
lore: A strange beast with a snake's head, leopard's body, and lion's legs.

name: Catoblepas
lore: A buffalo-like creature with a heavy head that always hangs down.

name: Leucrotta
lore: A hybrid of a hyena and a stag with a wide mouth reaching to its ears.

name: Yale
lore: An antelope-like beast with swiveling horns that can move independently.

name: Cockerel
lore: A hybrid bird, often associated with the Basilisk's birth or warding spirits.

name: Hippocamp
lore: A sea-horse with the forequarters of a horse and the tail of a fish.

name: Sea-Goat
lore: A creature with the upper body of a goat and the tail of a fish.

name: Ophiotaurus
lore: A hybrid with the front half of a bull and the tail of a serpent.

name: Musimon
lore: A hybrid of a goat and a sheep, possessing the horns of both.

name: Monoceros
lore: A stag-headed creature with a horse's body and a single spiral horn.

name: Bonnacon
lore: A bull-like creature with inward-curving horns that defends itself with caustic dung.

name: Lindworm
lore: A wingless, bipedal dragon with a long serpentine body.

name: Tarasque
lore: A six-legged beast with a lion's head, turtle's shell, and bear claws.

name: Vouivre
lore: A winged serpent or wyvern with a large ruby set into its forehead.

name: Culebre
lore: A giant winged serpent that guards treasures in caves.

name: Balaur
lore: A multi-headed dragon of enormous size that can control the weather.

name: Hydra
lore: A swamp-dwelling water serpent that regrows two heads for every one lost.

name: Zmey
lore: A Slavic dragon that can be benevolent protectors of crops or malicious.

name: Guivre
lore: A venomous serpentine dragon that lacks wings and legs.

name: Knucker
lore: A water dragon that lives in deep knuckerholes in the English countryside.

name: Lambton Worm
lore: A giant, legless serpent that coiled around a hill and terrorized a village.

name: Peluda
lore: A green, porcupine-like dragon that shoots poisonous quills.

name: Pukis
lore: A multi-headed household dragon that brings wealth to its owner.

name: Aitvaras (Serpent)
lore: A household spirit that appears as a fiery serpent in the sky.

name: Chuvash Dragon
lore: A shapeshifting dragon that can take human form to lure victims.

name: Ladōn
lore: A hundred-headed serpent that guarded the Golden Apples.

name: Python
lore: The earth-dragon of Delphi, slain by the god Apollo.

name: Bolsh
lore: A massive, destructive serpent from Balkan folklore.

name: Dragonet
lore: A smaller, often venomous species of regional dragon.

name: Drakon
lore: A giant, sharp-sighted serpent or dragon of Greek myth.

name: Fire-Drake
lore: A classic fire-breathing dragon inhabiting caves and hoarding gold.

name: Wulver
lore: A wolf-headed man who leaves fish on the windowsills of the poor.

name: Cu Sith
lore: A giant green hound that silences its victims with three barks.

name: Cadejo
lore: A spectral dog that can be either a protector or a curse.

name: Barghest
lore: A demonic black dog with huge teeth and claws.

name: Gallytrot
lore: A white, shadowy dog that chases travelers at night.

name: Moddey Dhoo
lore: A spectral black spaniel that haunts Peel Castle.

name: Skvader
lore: A Swedish hybrid with the forequarters of a hare and the back of a wood grouse.

name: Jackalope
lore: A hare with deer antlers; while famously American, it has roots in European horned hare lore.

name: Karkinos
lore: A giant crab that assisted the Hydra in its battle against Heracles.

name: Caladrius
lore: A snow-white bird that can predict if a sick person will recover by looking at them.

name: Stymphalian Bird
lore: Man-eating birds with metallic feathers and sharp brass beaks.

name: Salamander
lore: A lizard-like creature able to live in and extinguish fire.

name: Aspidochelone
lore: A sea monster so large its back is mistaken for an island with trees.

name: Fastitocalon
lore: A giant turtle-island monster that sinks when sailors light fires on its back.

name: Gulon
lore: A gluttonous creature with a cat-like head and a dog's body that eats incessantly.

name: Kudiani
lore: A shapeshifting sorceress or bird-spirit from Georgian legend.

name: Laukosargas
lore: A Lithuanian spirit that guards the fields and ensures a good harvest.

name: Baubas
lore: A dark, spindly-fingered spirit used to scare children into behaving.

name: Vadājs
lore: A forest spirit that leads travelers in circles until they are hopelessly lost.

name: Liche
lore: A skeletal, undead being that has retained its intelligence and magic.

name: Dahu
lore: A mountain goat with legs shorter on one side to better walk on slopes.

name: Matagot
lore: A black cat spirit that brings wealth if treated with great respect.

name: Wichtel
lore: Small, helpful house-elves in Germanic folklore that perform chores at night.

name: Heinzelmännchen
lore: Household gnomes from Cologne who did the work of lazy bakers.

name: Kobold
lore: Ambivalent spirits that can help in mines or cause domestic chaos.

name: Nix
lore: A shapeshifting water spirit that lures people to deep water with music.

name: Nökke
lore: A Scandinavian water spirit known for playing the violin to lure victims.

name: Vodyanoy
lore: A frog-like water spirit that causes floods and pulls people underwater.

name: Bolotnik
lore: A swamp spirit covered in algae who mimics the sounds of animals.

name: Polevik
lore: A field spirit with skin the color of soil and hair like grass.

name: Ovinnik
lore: A malevolent barn spirit that appears as a black cat with burning eyes.

name: Vila
lore: Beautiful mountain nymphs who can heal or shoot arrows at enemies.

name: Samovila
lore: Forest spirits in the Balkans who guard the waters and dance in circles.

name: Zmey Gorynych
lore: A three-headed Russian dragon that kidnaps women and burns villages.

name: Poludnica
lore: The Lady Midday who strikes those working in the fields during high noon.

name: Besta
lore: A Portuguese monstrous beast often used as a general term for demons.
